title: Reading before writing deterministic misconception check

publication.changeSummary: Deterministic misconception item for Reading before writing; blocked from formal exams until independent human review.

items.title: Reading before writing: correct the failure model
items.prompt: Which statement correctly resolves this documented beginner mistake? Understanding code means reading each line's syntax; predicting state and behavior is unnecessary until editing begins.

examEligibility.rationale: The deterministic answer and rubric are AI-assisted draft content awaiting independent human approval.

hints: Compare both options with the canonical model, declared boundary, and observable evidence in the lesson.

feedback.correct: Correct. Build and test a behavioral model first, because purpose, data flow, effects, and edge cases determine whether a later edit preserves the contract.
feedback.incorrect: The selected statement preserves the documented misconception. Build and test a behavioral model first, because purpose, data flow, effects, and edge cases determine whether a later edit preserves the contract.

criteria.description: Selects the source-aligned correction for Reading before writing instead of the documented misconception.

privateAuthorNotes: Human reviewer must verify the correction against the cited source and ensure the misconception option is plausible without reinforcing it.
